objective: Measure whether the Wave 47/48 13-row value-unique exact support limit is a greedy selector artifact or a current component-value capacity ceiling.
non_negotiables: This is an upper-bound diagnostic, not a promoted shard.
The report must keep the current selected row count separate from the component-value capacity upper bound.
Future exact support claims must increase fresh component-value supply or document a different value/split rule.
Stop after this wave and push the completed commits.

tasks.prompt: Add a value-unique signature upper-bound report that counts current left/right unique component values, combined value capacity, candidate value-pair counts, and current greedy selector output.
outputs: Regression test for the component-value ceiling
acceptance: The report records 15 left and 14 right unique component value digests.
The report records component_value_capacity_upper_bound=14 and current_selected_row_count=13.
The report does not run expensive all-pairs thermograph result digest calculations.

tasks.prompt: Run the Wave 49 upper-bound report and record the hash.
Record Wave 49 as evidence that the current exact support limit is near the component-value capacity ceiling.
acceptance: Docs state that new component-value diversity is required before exact support can scale materially.
Docs do not present Wave 49 as a new shard or model result.

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_49_value_unique_signature_upper_bound.json
integration_gates.success_signal: The Wave 49 plan is internally consistent.
The current source is capacity-limited by component-value diversity.
